Anti-virus software uses various techniques to detect and prevent malicious software from infecting a computer system. These techniques include signature-based detection, which looks for known patterns of malware in files and network traffic; heuristics-based detection, which analyzes the behavior of software to identify potentially malicious activity; and sandboxing, which isolates potentially dangerous software in a controlled environment to study its behavior.

Program: AI Anti-Virus Evasion Tool

Overview:
The purpose of this program is to use artificial intelligence techniques to evade detection by anti-virus software. It does so by continuously learning and adapting to the behavior of the anti-virus software, allowing it to modify its code and avoid being flagged as malicious. The tool generates a safety report after running for a set period or achieving a specific goal, which can be customized by the user.

Languages: C++, Python (for machine learning)

Features:

1. Code obfuscation: Obfuscate the code to make it harder for anti-virus software
